visa_types.name: Business, Conference, Cultural Or Sport Event
visa_types.documents_required:
Travel insurance is mandatory for all Schengen countries. If you have not purchased, please click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/idn/en/cze/travel-insurance).
Please be aware that all supporting documents submitted along with the visa application are kept at the Embassy.
Applications for Schengen Visas to the Embassy of Czech Republic in Jakarta can be submitted, by scheduling an appointment and solely by personal appearance of the applicant, to the VFS Czech Republic Visa Application Centre, only on Monday, Wednesday and friday, from 08:00 to 12:00. Applicants who have rendered their fingerprint data in the last 59 months may opt to delegate the submission to a third party (kindly note that the Embassy reserve the rights to call the applicant(s) if the biometric/fingerprint data cannot be retrieved in the system).
Kindly refer to the list of requisite documents below before submitting your application; this will minimize chances of rejection of your Visa Application due to incorrect or inadequate
                                                                        documents.
List of documents required for Business/Conference/Sport (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/3tm8TbdafVcHnEfpEZEq5n/a2429a463abed236568c105fce45493b/New-Checklist-for-a-Short-Schengen-Visa-Czech-Republic-revise-version-10.01.pdf)
References for insurance companies
visa_types.visa_fees:
The Visa Fees are as follows:

[cze > idn > en > Table:Business, Conference, Cultural Or Sport Event > Visa Fees 1]
Note:
A service charge of Indonesian Rupiah (IDR) 419.000 is levied per applicant by VFS over and above the Visa Fee, is payable in cash, debit, credit, transfer or QRIS in Indonesian Rupiah only. Visa fees are collected on behalf of the Embassy of Czech Republic in Jakarta and shall not be chargeable to any tax in Indonesia. All VAS and services fees are inclusive of VAT.
An official receipt should be obtained for all payments made. Since pandemic courier service was charged as mandatory.
Please note that the visa fees are as above indicated. No other amount will be requested for the visa issuance or for urgent process for visa issuance.
Terms and Conditions:
Kindly be informed that Logistic fee and other additional service fees are not refundable once the payment is made.
visa_types.processing_time:
Please note that as per the Schengen Visa Code, the standard processing time of a short-term Schengen visa application is up to 15 calendar days from reaching the Embassy (not from the date of lodging a visa application at a visa application center).This period may be extended up to a maximum of 45 calendar days in individual cases, notably when further scrutiny of the application is needed. It is strongly recommended to apply at least 3 weeks prior the planned departure. Please be sure to submit your visa application well in advance, to avoid any inconveniences. 
Please be sure to submit your visa application well in advance, to avoid any inconveniences.
All visa decisions are solely at the discretion of the Embassy of Czech Republic in Jakarta.
visa_types.overview:
Overview
Application is applicable in case you are travelling for business. This visa is given for a travel period of less than 90 days.
Every applicant, including children/infants, has to submit his/her Schengen visa application in person and by appointment only on their appointment date, which needs to be booked online
                                                                        see Schedule an Appointment by the applicant.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
Application is applicable in case you are travelling for business. This visa is given for a travel period of less than 90 days.
Every applicant, including children/infants, has to submit his/her Schengen visa application in person and by appointment only on their appointment date, which needs to be booked online
                                                                        see Schedule an Appointment by the applicant.

## Visa Fees
The Visa Fees are as follows:

[cze > idn > en > Table:Business, Conference, Cultural Or Sport Event > Visa Fees 1]
Note:
A service charge of Indonesian Rupiah (IDR) 419.000 is levied per applicant by VFS over and above the Visa Fee, is payable in cash, debit, credit, transfer or QRIS in Indonesian Rupiah only. Visa fees are collected on behalf of the Embassy of Czech Republic in Jakarta and shall not be chargeable to any tax in Indonesia. All VAS and services fees are inclusive of VAT.
An official receipt should be obtained for all payments made. Since pandemic courier service was charged as mandatory.
Please note that the visa fees are as above indicated. No other amount will be requested for the visa issuance or for urgent process for visa issuance.
Terms and Conditions:
Kindly be informed that Logistic fee and other additional service fees are not refundable once the payment is made.

## Documents Required
Travel insurance is mandatory for all Schengen countries. If you have not purchased, please click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/idn/en/cze/travel-insurance).
Please be aware that all supporting documents submitted along with the visa application are kept at the Embassy.
Applications for Schengen Visas to the Embassy of Czech Republic in Jakarta can be submitted, by scheduling an appointment and solely by personal appearance of the applicant, to the VFS Czech Republic Visa Application Centre, only on Monday, Wednesday and friday, from 08:00 to 12:00. Applicants who have rendered their fingerprint data in the last 59 months may opt to delegate the submission to a third party (kindly note that the Embassy reserve the rights to call the applicant(s) if the biometric/fingerprint data cannot be retrieved in the system).
Kindly refer to the list of requisite documents below before submitting your application; this will minimize chances of rejection of your Visa Application due to incorrect or inadequate
                                                                        documents.
List of documents required for Business/Conference/Sport (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/3tm8TbdafVcHnEfpEZEq5n/a2429a463abed236568c105fce45493b/New-Checklist-for-a-Short-Schengen-Visa-Czech-Republic-revise-version-10.01.pdf)
References for insurance companies

## Photo Specifications
Please provide 1 recent colour photographs, meeting the following requirements:
- Taken against a light (white or light grey) background so that features are distinguishable and contrast against the background.
- Clear quality and the face should cover at least 80% of the picture
- Size 3,5 x 4,5 cm
If the photographs presented do not meet these requirements (see examples here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/2P2LtCIp18kC8fV10b3cVL/c6ab4c399f47de43152c57ec0f5ad977/photo-specification.pdf)), then the visa application will
                                                                        be considered incomplete and applicant will be asked to take new photographs in the photo booth in the visa application center.

## Processing Time
Please note that as per the Schengen Visa Code, the standard processing time of a short-term Schengen visa application is up to 15 calendar days from reaching the Embassy (not from the date of lodging a visa application at a visa application center).This period may be extended up to a maximum of 45 calendar days in individual cases, notably when further scrutiny of the application is needed. It is strongly recommended to apply at least 3 weeks prior the planned departure. Please be sure to submit your visa application well in advance, to avoid any inconveniences. 
Please be sure to submit your visa application well in advance, to avoid any inconveniences.
All visa decisions are solely at the discretion of the Embassy of Czech Republic in Jakarta.

## Download
Below is a form that is required to be filled to submit your visa application:
- Schengen Visa Application Form.

visa_types.overview:
Overview
Airport transit visa is issued for one or two entries. Its maximum duration is six months.
Airport  transit visa is  issued to persons transiting through one of the Czech international airports if  they are citizens/ holders of travel documents of the following states:
                                                                        Afghanistan, Algeria, Armenia, Bangladesh, Chad, Congo, Cuba, Egypt, Eritrea, Ethiopia, Ghana, India, Iran, Iraq, Lebanon, Libya, Mali, Mauretania, Nigeria, Niger, Pakistan, Palestinian
                                                                        National Authority, Somalia, South Sudan, Sri Langa, Sudan, Syria, Turkey, Yemen. 
Following  categories of persons are exempt from the requirement to apply for an airport  transit visa:
- holders of a
Schengen       (short-stay) visa (https://www.mzv.cz/jnp/en/information_for_aliens/short_stay_visa/index.html)
- holders of a
long-stay       visa or long-term residence permit (https://www.mzv.cz/jnp/en/information_for_aliens/long_stay_visa/index.html)
- holders of a residence permit in Andorra, Canada, Japan, San Marino and the United States
- holders of a visa for stay in the states of the EEA and in Canada, Japan and the United States
- family members of EU citizens
- holders of diplomatic passports
- crew members who are citizens of States Parties of the ICAO
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
Airport transit visa is issued for one or two entries. Its maximum duration is six months.
Airport  transit visa is  issued to persons transiting through one of the Czech international airports if  they are citizens/ holders of travel documents of the following states:
                                                                        Afghanistan, Algeria, Armenia, Bangladesh, Chad, Congo, Cuba, Egypt, Eritrea, Ethiopia, Ghana, India, Iran, Iraq, Lebanon, Libya, Mali, Mauretania, Nigeria, Niger, Pakistan, Palestinian
                                                                        National Authority, Somalia, South Sudan, Sri Langa, Sudan, Syria, Turkey, Yemen. 
Following  categories of persons are exempt from the requirement to apply for an airport  transit visa:
- holders of a
Schengen       (short-stay) visa (https://www.mzv.cz/jnp/en/information_for_aliens/short_stay_visa/index.html)
- holders of a
long-stay       visa or long-term residence permit (https://www.mzv.cz/jnp/en/information_for_aliens/long_stay_visa/index.html)
- holders of a residence permit in Andorra, Canada, Japan, San Marino and the United States
- holders of a visa for stay in the states of the EEA and in Canada, Japan and the United States
- family members of EU citizens
- holders of diplomatic passports
- crew members who are citizens of States Parties of the ICAO

## Visa Fees
The Visa Fees are as follows:

[cze > idn > en > Table:Tourism/ Medical Treatment > Visa Fees 1]
Note:
A service charge of Indonesian Rupiah (IDR) 419.000 is levied per applicant by VFS over and above the Visa Fee, is payable in cash, debit, credit, transfer or QRIS in Indonesian Rupiah only. Visa fees are collected on behalf of the Embassy of Czech Republic in Jakarta and shall not be chargeable to any tax in Indonesia. All VAS and services fees are inclusive of VAT.
An official receipt should be obtained for all payments made. Since pandemic courier service was charged as mandatory.
Please note that the visa fees are as above indicated. No other amount will be requested for the visa issuance or for urgent process for visa issuance.
Terms and Conditions:
Kindly be informed that Logistic fee and other additional service fees are not refundable once the payment is made.

## Documents Required
Travel insurance is mandatory for all Schengen countries. If you have not purchased, please click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/idn/en/cze/travel-insurance).
Please be aware that all supporting documents submitted along with the visa application are kept at the Embassy.

## DOCUMENTS REQUIRED FOR ALL APPLICANTS
Applications for Schengen Visas to the Embassy of Czech Republic in Jakarta can be submitted, by scheduling an appointment and solely by personal appearance of the applicant, to the VFS Czech Republic Visa Application Centre, only on Monday, Wednesday and friday, from 08:00 to 12:00. Applicants who have rendered their fingerprint data in the last 59 months may opt to delegate the submission to a third party (kindly note that the Embassy reserve the rights to call the applicant(s) if the biometric/fingerprint data cannot be retrieved in the system).
Kindly refer to the list of requisite documents below before submitting your application; this will minimize chances of rejection of your Visa Application due to incorrect or inadequate
                                                                        documents.
List of documents required for Airport Transit (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/3tm8TbdafVcHnEfpEZEq5n/a2429a463abed236568c105fce45493b/New-Checklist-for-a-Short-Schengen-Visa-Czech-Republic-revise-version-10.01.pdf)
References for insurance companies

## Photo Specifications
Please provide 1 recent colour photographs, meeting the following requirements:
- Taken against a light (white or light grey) background so that features are distinguishable and contrast against the background.
- Clear quality and the face should cover at least 80% of the picture
- Size 3,5 x 4,5 cm
If the photographs presented do not meet these requirements (see examples here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/2P2LtCIp18kC8fV10b3cVL/c6ab4c399f47de43152c57ec0f5ad977/photo-specification.pdf)), then the visa application will
                                                                        be considered incomplete and applicant will be asked to take new photographs in the photo booth in the visa application center.

## Processing Time
Please note that as per the Schengen Visa Code, the standard processing time of a short-term Schengen visa application is up to 15 calendar days from reaching the Embassy (not from the date of lodging a visa application at a visa application center).This period may be extended up to a maximum of 45 calendar days in individual cases, notably when further scrutiny of the application is needed. It is strongly recommended to apply at least 3 weeks prior the planned departure. Please be sure to submit your visa application well in advance, to avoid any inconveniences. 
Please be sure to submit your visa application well in advance, to avoid any inconveniences.
All visa decisions are solely at the discretion of the Embassy of Czech Republic in Jakarta.

## Download
Below is a form that is required to be filled to submit your visa application:
- Schengen Visa Application Form.

visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
Schengen visa for the purpose of training.
Every applicant has to submit his/her Schengen visa application in person and by appointment only on their appointment date, which needs to be booked online see Schedule an Appointment
                                                                        by the applicant.

## Visa Fees
The Visa Fees are as follows:

[cze > idn > en > Table:Tourism/ Medical Treatment > Visa Fees 1]
Note:
A service charge of Indonesian Rupiah (IDR) 419.000 is levied per applicant by VFS over and above the Visa Fee, is payable in cash, debit, credit, transfer or QRIS in Indonesian Rupiah only. Visa fees are collected on behalf of the Embassy of Czech Republic in Jakarta and shall not be chargeable to any tax in Indonesia. All VAS and services fees are inclusive of VAT.
An official receipt should be obtained for all payments made. Since pandemic courier service was charged as mandatory.
Please note that the visa fees are as above indicated. No other amount will be requested for the visa issuance or for urgent process for visa issuance.
Terms and Conditions:
Kindly be informed that Logistic fee and other additional service fees are not refundable once the payment is made.

## Documents Required
Travel insurance is mandatory for all Schengen countries. If you have not purchased, please click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/idn/en/cze/travel-insurance).
Please be aware that all supporting documents submitted along with the visa application are kept at the Embassy.
Applications for Schengen Visas to the Embassy of Czech Republic in Jakarta can be submitted, by scheduling an appointment and solely by personal appearance of the applicant, to the VFS Czech Republic Visa Application Centre, only on Monday, Wednesday and friday, from 08:00 to 12:00. Applicants who have rendered their fingerprint data in the last 59 months may opt to delegate the submission to a third party (kindly note that the Embassy reserve the rights to call the applicant(s) if the biometric/fingerprint data cannot be retrieved in the system).
Kindly refer to the list of requisite documents below before submitting your application; this will minimize chances of rejection of your Visa Application due to incorrect or inadequate
                                                                        documents.
List of documents required for Training (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/3tm8TbdafVcHnEfpEZEq5n/a2429a463abed236568c105fce45493b/New-Checklist-for-a-Short-Schengen-Visa-Czech-Republic-revise-version-10.01.pdf)
References for insurance companies

## Photo Specifications
Please provide 1 recent colour photographs, meeting the following requirements:
- Taken against a light (white or light grey) background so that features are distinguishable and contrast against the background.
- Clear quality and the face should cover at least 80% of the picture
- Size 3,5 x 4,5 cm
If the photographs presented do not meet these requirements (see examples here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/2P2LtCIp18kC8fV10b3cVL/c6ab4c399f47de43152c57ec0f5ad977/photo-specification.pdf)), then the visa application will
                                                                        be considered incomplete and applicant will be asked to take new photographs in the photo booth in the visa application center.

## Processing Time
Please note that as per the Schengen Visa Code, the standard processing time of a short-term Schengen visa application is up to 15 calendar days from reaching the Embassy (not from the date of lodging a visa application at a visa application center).This period may be extended up to a maximum of 45 calendar days in individual cases, notably when further scrutiny of the application is needed. It is strongly recommended to apply at least 3 weeks prior the planned departure. Please be sure to submit your visa application well in advance, to avoid any inconveniences. 
Please be sure to submit your visa application well in advance, to avoid any inconveniences.
All visa decisions are solely at the discretion of the Embassy of Czech Republic in Jakarta.

## Download
Below is a form that is required to be filled to submit your visa application:
- Schengen Visa Application Form.
